name: Curmudgeon Pickled Hash a La Al Martin
recipeIngredient: 10 gallons Red Wine Vinegar or Ripple
recipeInstructions:
Squid should be at least bait quality if fresh. If squid are not available, use other suitable seafood.
Tofu should be freshly made to Al's personal specifications.
Al's Secret Curmudgeon Sauce contains 1 quart Sweat Sauce, 1 quart Nam Pla (Fish Sauce), 1 quart Wednesday's Frosties, 1 gallon Justin Wilson's Dehydrator Barbecue Sauce and six dozen $250.00 cookies.
- Remove skins from Waller Walla onions.
- Wash squid, remove skin and entrails, leaving tentacles.
- Carefully cube tofu into exact 1-inch cubes (Al is particular about this).
- Set squid aside in the sun while preparing brine/pickling mixture.
- In a 55-gallon oak barrel, combine red wine vinegar, water, salt, sugar, Tabasco Sauce and Secret Curmudgeon Sauce; mix thoroughly.
- Add minced garlic (16 pounds per batch), pickling spices, crushed red pepper and stir until well mixed.
- Place onions, squid and tofu into the brine solution making sure everything is submerged. If necessary, place a manhole cover on top to keep ingredients submerged.
- Allow the process to continue as long as possible without sampling. It should be ready by picnic time. That's all there is to it. Named after the Patron Curmudgeon of the Cooking Echo - Al Martin.
